blocks.text: Melissa Johnson, Jason Torres still copied:

Thank you for turning the service tickets around so quickly. They arrived last night and they already answer part of the question I had. Against the online orders at {{fact:f:E-2011-001.client}}, the complaints cluster in the day after an order is placed, and most of them are people asking where their order is, not people reporting something wrong with it. That points at the hand-off again rather than at the fulfillment itself, and it is a useful thing to be able to say with the ticket data behind it instead of on impression alone.

One follow-up while your team still has the files open. Where a ticket was escalated, I would like to see who it went to, because that shows where the channel's problems land when no one owns them by design. Even a rough note against a dozen recent escalations would do; I do not need the whole quarter marked up.

Jason and I will have the order-path map and this first cut of the service picture ready for the working session next week. If the escalation notes are difficult to pull, say so and we will work from what we already have.

Deborah Allen
